title: Regional travel and season-two approach phase
summary: Thirty-sixth structured packet for Eco Explorer. This is a lane-1 follow-on after the directional travel coherence phase: first strengthen map-return posts and one walking-only regional approach cue, then tighten the logged expedition footer into one calm region-led next-season invitation without opening a larger navigation UI or route catalog.

execution_order.goal: Prepare regional travel and map-return post guidance.
execution_order.why_now: Once directional travel is coherent, the next travel improvement should be one stronger sense of regional approach.

execution_order.goal: Add one compact regional pack: region-labeled map-return posts plus a walking-only approach cue.
execution_order.why_now: This is the smallest follow-on that makes the world feel more chapter-like without another map UI.

execution_order.goal: Review the regional travel and approach pass.
execution_order.why_now: The pass should stay calm and subtle.
After travel is clearer, one tiny outward invitation is the next best lane-1 payoff.

execution_order.goal: Shorten the logged expedition teaser into one calm region-led second-season invitation.
execution_order.why_now: The regional travel pass already gave the map its new vocabulary, so the next move is to make the station echo that outward direction without another surface.
Lane 1 should only keep this if it stays light and notebook-like.

guardrails: Do not add a larger map HUD or navigation layer.
Keep the work after directional coherence, not before it.
Prefer spatial framing and tiny cues over more copy.
Use the existing logged expedition teaser as the invitation seam instead of adding another station panel.
Do not replace the current corridor destination labels; they already carry the directional load.
Treat the already-landed regional map labels and approach cues as the map side of the invitation.
acceptance_focus: Travel feels more regionally grounded after the coherence pass.
The station and map point gently toward the next season.
Lane 1 stays compact and avoids a larger navigation shell.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-1.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-regional-travel-and-season-two-approach-phase.md, and this packet. Treat this as a future lane-1 follow-on after travel coherence: add subtle regional approach cues, calmer map-return posts, and one tiny outward invitation without opening a larger map UI.
